relations.subject: Australian bustard
relations.evidence: This bustard will enter denser areas of vegetation after fire, and is observed on artificial cleared areas such as golf courses and farmland. The historical range in Southwest Australia included the dunes and alluvial flats of the Swan Coastal Plain, as far south as Busselton, and at the interior in what became the Wheatbelt

relations.subject: Australian bustard
relations.evidence: The greatest fidelity to a local region is reported in areas of high rainfall in the north and northwest of Australia. The habitat favoured by the species is open grasslands, perhaps with some trees, spinifex plains and low shrublands
relations.object_summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

relations.subject: Australian bustard
relations.evidence: thumb|Ardeotis australis in front of a Bushfires in Australia|bushfire This bird remains relatively common and widespread across most of northern Australia (see Atlas), but its range appears to have contracted in the southeast of the continent during the last century, perhaps due to hunting (now illegal except for indigenous Australians), feral predators such as pigs and foxes (Vulpes vulpes) and habitat destruction
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.object_summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。
